summary_one_line: Reaching the SAX-guard FALSE side in a big-buffer char-data flush, which requires disableSAX==1 reached via a distinct error-then-content call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser into xmlParseCharDataComplex with a >=XML_PARSER_BIG_BUFFER_SIZE character run while ctxt->disableSAX is true (a prior fatal error has set disableSAX==1), so the guard (sax!=NULL && !disableSAX) at L4653 evaluates FALSE; the well-formed DOCTYPE-bearing XML of the winner seeds reaches this state through a richer set of caller call-contexts than the malformed loser inputs.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed XML beginning '<?xml version="1.0"?>' with a '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' header, exercising clean parser states that let naive_ctx's context-indexed feedback retain many distinct (call-context, edge) corpus entries reaching the blocker; the divergent-branch dump shows the winner does take the FALSE side (W L4653 T=0 F=2) which the loser never reaches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup (e.g. 'ht~p://-/' and long runs of repeated bytes) that stall on the TRUE side only (L L4653 T=16 F=0); plain naive's bare-edge feedback collapses the many call paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex onto the same edge slots, so it never retains a corpus path that arrives at the big-buffer flush with disableSAX==1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx and naive share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S or CMP_MAP; the only differing technique is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller-chain hash into the edge-map index so the same blocker edge counts as new coverage when reached through a new call context. This context granularity preserves more diverse paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex, eventually surfacing an input whose disableSAX state flips the L4653 guard FALSE.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the FALSE side of the SAX guard at parser.c:4653 inside xmlParseCharDataComplex.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line: 	    if ((ctxt->sax != NULL) && (!ctxt->disableSAX)) {
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is naive_ctx beating naive, with the ctx_coverage technique delta.
The winner takes the previously-blocked FALSE side of L4653 while the loser only ever takes TRUE.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4653  T=0 F=2  T=16 F=0  if ((ctxt->sax != NULL) && (!ctxt->disableSAX)) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed XML with an XML declaration and DOCTYPE SYSTEM header.
Loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup rather than clean XML.
naive_ctx uses the same mutators as naive with no I2S or CMP_MAP; the only added technique is the context-hash folded into the edge-map index.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
keeps a running hash of the current call context (caller chain) and
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: plain naive has no I2S substitution, confirming the divergence is not a comparison-solving effect.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the db_query.py lineage I2S-floor check is not mandatory. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms both naive and naive_ctx share an identical havoc+token stack with no I2SRandReplace and no CMP_MAP, isolating the CtxHook context-indexed edge map as the sole technique delta; no dash-row lineage verification applies here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness reaching the same big-buffer SAX-guard FALSE side where plain naive resolves the branch as fast as naive_ctx, showing the bare edge map is sufficient and call-context granularity is not what gates the FALSE side.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact program-state path that sets ctxt->disableSAX==1 before the big-buffer flush is inferred from the disableSAX==1 branches in the call chain rather than directly observed in the winner seed's execution.
